quality.rationale: 基于偏导数链式法则证明坐标变换雅可比矩阵的乘法闭合性，逻辑严密且适用于任意光滑流形背景

origin.question: Problem 3.10. If $\Lambda_{\beta}^{a}$ and $\tilde{\Lambda}_{\beta}^{a}$ are two matrices which transform the components of a tensor from one coordinate basis to another, show that the matrix $\Lambda_{\gamma}^{a}\tilde{\Lambda}_{\beta}^{\gamma}$ is also a coordinate transformation.
origin.answer: The product matrix $\Lambda_{\gamma}^{\alpha} \tilde{\Lambda}_{\beta}^{\gamma}$ satisfies the partial derivative chain rule and therefore constitutes a valid composite coordinate transformation matrix.
origin.solution: Solution 3.10. Suppose one coordinate transformation is$$\bar {\mathbf {x}}^{\mu} = \bar {\mathbf {x}}^{\mu} \left(\mathbf {x}^{\nu}\right), \quad \text {s o} \quad \Lambda_{\beta}^{\alpha} = \partial_{\bar {\mathbf {x}}}^{\alpha} / \partial_{\mathbf {x}}^{\beta} \tag {1}$$and the other is$$\tilde {\mathbf {x}}^{\mu} = \tilde {\mathbf {x}}^{\mu} \left(\mathbf {x}^{\nu}\right), \quad \text {s o} \quad \tilde{\Lambda}_{\beta}^{\alpha} = \partial_{\tilde {\mathbf {x}}}^{\alpha} / \partial_{\mathbf {x}}^{\beta}. \tag {2}$$The product matrix is then$$\Lambda_{\gamma}^{\alpha} \tilde{\Lambda}_{\beta}^{\gamma} = \frac {\partial_{\overline {{\mathbf {x}}}}^{\alpha}}{\partial_{\mathbf {x}}^{\gamma}} \frac {\partial_{\tilde {\mathbf {x}}}^{\gamma}}{\partial_{\mathbf {x}} \beta}. \tag {3}$$This looks suspiciously like the chain rule for partial derivatives, so one is led to consider the coordinate transformation$$\overline {{\mathbf {x}}}^{\mu} (\mathbf {x}^{\beta}) = \overline {{\mathbf {x}}}^{\mu} \left[ \tilde {\mathbf {x}}^{\gamma} (\mathbf {x}^{\beta})\right], \tag {4}$$whose transformation matrix is obviously$$\bar {\Lambda}_{\beta}^{\mu} = \frac {\partial_{\overline {{\mathbf {x}}}}^{\mu}}{\partial_{\mathbf {x}} \beta} = \frac {\partial_{\overline {{\mathbf {x}}}}^{\mu}}{\partial_{\widetilde {\mathbf {x}}}^{\gamma}} \frac {\partial_{\widetilde {\mathbf {x}}}^{\gamma}}{\partial_{\mathbf {x}} \beta}. \tag {5}$$This differs from (3) only by a twiddle. A little thought shows that the twiddle is not meaningful. Partial derivatives are taken with respect to argument variables; it makes no difference what symbols are used to represent these variables. Thus (4) is in fact the coordinate transformation of the product matrix.
